title: Eye Rendering — Cornea, Iris & Caustics
description: Covers the physically based eye rendering model: cornea geometry with a separate refractive layer above the iris surface, limbal ring darkening, procedural iris detail using a polar noise texture, pupil dilation animation, and the real-time eye caustic generated by a pre-computed LUT indexed by light-to-pupil direction. Eyes are among the most scrutinized features in character rendering; physically plausible eye rendering requires a dedicated multi-layer shading model not present in standard material systems. Students will implement the dual-layer cornea+iris eye model in HLSL, add limbal ring and moisture highlight as separate rendering layers, and integrate the eye caustic LUT for key lighting scenarios.
